svg.text: AnimaMesh — the engine and the instance
Two repos, a one-way dependency. The public engine is generic logic; each private brain owns its knowledge, configuration, deploy workspace, and evidence.
anima-mesh — the ENGINE (public, on GitHub)
Generic logic that animates any brain. Never names a
company, person, or persona — identity is configuration.
src/ — the logic
harness/ — heartbeat + direction cores; prompt assembly
providers/ — the model chokepoint (fetch + subprocess tiers)
sources/ — opted-in read-only context (Graph · GitHub · local)
gates/ + autonomy/ — the L1–L4 ladder, enforced in code
ledger/ + okf/ — append-only audit + the bundle format
instance/ — the storage seam: store-fs · store-github
channels/ + a2a/ — Discord/Gmail/Notion + agent card
init/ + cli.ts — scaffold · validate · run · gate · deliver
Safety lives here in deterministic code: gates, ladder, ledger,
and verifiers. Judgment between wake-up and gate belongs to models.
workers/ — the cloud tier (deployed per instance)
heartbeat/ — daily beat + inbound directions (2 DOs)
web/ — the Google-OIDC observability dashboard
templates/ + docs/
templates/agents/ — the roster, with {{PLACEHOLDER}} identity
docs/ — architecture, operator runbooks, platform learnings
The one hard rule
No private instance identity, real email, secret, or persona ever
appears in engine code, templates, tests, or docs.
A generally useful capability is promoted here de-identified:
placeholders for identity, configuration for choices, and
behavioral tests for every safety property.
Upgraded deliberately: released as a git tag vX.Y.Z.
your brain — the INSTANCE (private, yours)
One organization's back office as plain files. All real data.
Pins the engine by tag; stays private forever.
animamesh.config.json — the pairing
engine.ref · identity/persona · activation gates · A2A
cognition.overrides · delivery channels · direction agent
channel allowlists, budgets, and polling are Worker env controls
bundle/ — the knowledge (one concept per file)
constitution.md — immutable hard limits (machine-read)
facts/ — stable facts (status: verified / unverified)
decisions/ — dated, immutable; supersede, never rewrite
events/ — append-only; corrections are new events
agents/*.md — level · model · harness · sources opt-in
ops/calendar.md — what agents wake up to check
ops/nags.md — reminders that repeat until done
index.md · log.md — reserved (log is append-only)
read sources are external context; they never become write channels
durable evidence + deploy workspace
ledger/actions.jsonl — append-only action ledger
approvals/ · reports/ · drafts/ — gates and run artifacts
cloud/ — this instance's non-secret wrangler deploy configs
cloud beats and direction drains land as one commit each
credentials stay outside git
local: .env / .env.local (model, source, channel tokens)
cloud: Worker secrets (wrangler secret put)
code and tracked config reference names, never secret values
The engine reaches the same brain through store-fs locally or
store-github in the cloud — one contract, two transports.
depends on
pins engine @vX.Y.Z
generalize
promote, de-identified
one engine,
many isolated brains
instance A
instance B
The engine is pinned code, not a shared service. Its safety and seams upgrade independently of every private instance.
The brain owns knowledge and durable evidence; external sources remain read-only inputs selected per agent.
Both runtimes use the storage seam. The CLI writes the local clone; the cloud Worker commits through GitHub, so local work starts with git pull --rebase.
